operation: First, a 2D sketch is generated by combining a 5-unit by 5-unit rectangle and a 3-unit radius circle in the same 2D plane. The entire sketch is then translated to a new location offset 10 units along the X-axis, 10 units along the Y-axis, with 0 Z-axis offset. This moved sketch is placed on the XY workplane, then extruded 1 unit along the Z-axis to form the final 3D solid part.
geometry: This is a thin, uniform-thickness prism with a cross-section that is the boolean union of a square and a larger-diameter circle. It has a rounded central body with four small pointed protrusions extending outwards at 90-degree intervals around its perimeter. Its top and bottom faces are identical parallel flat planes, and all side faces are perpendicular to these top and bottom surfaces.
function: This custom profiled thin flat part is designed to be used as a spacer, gasket, mounting shim, or decorative emblem, with its unique profile tailored to fit specific alignment, sealing, or aesthetic requirements in an assembly.
